name: cep_not_flush_bit_preblocker
drl: rule NE when not E0() P() then end
_finding: D-153 (bit-semantics re-confirmation, nb880x7): unlinkNotNodeOnRightInsert fires only WHILE LINKED — a blocker arriving BEFORE any P (join empty => segment unlinked) leaves the not-bit SET, so the first P's staging LINKS the segment and queues fire 1: the IF is RESIDENT (blocked{()}) and drains are per-window. ep2's delete-unblock then emits at ITS eval over the move-to-tail-adjusted rtm: [1,3,4,2] + fresh [5,6]. A pure-emptiness bit model wrongly merges the windows (predicts [5,6,1,2,3,4]).
